name: Mesquite-Grilled Breast of Chicken with Citrus Sauce
recipeIngredient: 4 Chicken breasts (skinless, boneless, about ½ lb each)
2 Lemons (the rind, pith, and membrane cut away with a serrated knife and discarded; fruit chopped, reserving any juice)
2 Limes (the rind, pith, and membrane cut away with a serrated knife and discarded; fruit chopped, reserving any juice)
2 Oranges (the rind, pith, and membrane cut away with a serrated knife and discarded; fruit chopped, reserving any juice)
recipeInstructions:
1. Skinless, boneless chicken breasts (about ½ lb each), halved
2. In a ceramic or glass bowl, combine the oil, garlic, coriander, thyme, scallions, and chicken breasts. Let marinate, chilled, for at least 8 hours or overnight.
3. In a saucepan, cook the shallots and ginger in butter over moderately low heat, stirring until shallots are softened. Add chopped lemons, limes, oranges with all reserved juices; simmer mixture for 10-12 minutes, reducing to syrup-like consistency. Increase heat to moderate and boil mixture, stirring occasionally, for 10-15 minutes until reduced by one-third.
4. Mix cornstarch and water together. Stir into sauce, simmer briefly, strain through a fine sieve into bowl. Add Grand Marnier, white pepper, and salt to taste.
5. Remove chicken from marinade and let stand at room temperature for 15 minutes. Grill on rack about 5 inches above mesquite coals, turning once, until just cooked through, about 10-12 minutes. Transfer chicken to platter, spoon sauce over it, garnish with lemon wedges.
